claimText: The RBI Integrated Ombudsman Scheme covers Commercial Banks, Regional Rural Banks (RRBs), Urban Co-operative Banks with a deposit size of ₹50 crore or more, and Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) with an asset size of ₹100 crore or more.
evidenceExcerpt: Covers Commercial Banks, RRBs, Urban Co-operative Banks (deposit size ₹50 crore+), NBFCs (asset size ₹100 crore+), System Participants, and Credit Information Companies.
examUse: Statement-based question to test knowledge of the institutions and their specific eligibility criteria covered under the scheme.
distractorHooks: Incorrect asset/deposit thresholds for NBFCs and Co-operative Banks.
Stating all NBFCs or all Co-operative banks are covered regardless of size.
Using asset size for Co-operative banks or deposit size for NBFCs.

claimText: The RBI Integrated Ombudsman Scheme integrates three previous schemes: the Banking Ombudsman Scheme (2006), the Ombudsman Scheme for NBFCs (2018), and the Ombudsman Scheme for Digital Transactions (2019).
evidenceExcerpt: Integrates the Banking Ombudsman Scheme (2006), Ombudsman Scheme for NBFCs (2018), and Ombudsman Scheme for Digital Transactions (2019).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question asking which schemes were merged to form the integrated scheme.
distractorHooks: Including other unrelated ombudsman schemes (e.g., Insurance Ombudsman).
Stating it only replaced one or two of the mentioned schemes.

claimText: The RBI Integrated Ombudsman Scheme operates on a 'One Nation One Ombudsman' principle, making it jurisdiction-neutral.
evidenceExcerpt: Adopts a ‘One Nation One Ombudsman’ approach, making it jurisdiction neutral.
examUse: Conceptual question about the operational philosophy or jurisdictional nature of the scheme.
distractorHooks: Confusing the principle with a single physical office for the entire country.
Stating that complaints must be filed based on the bank's head office location.

claimText: The Ombudsman under the integrated scheme can award compensation up to ₹20 lakh, with an additional provision for up to ₹1 lakh for the complainant's expenses and mental distress.
evidenceExcerpt: Compensation can be up to ₹20 lakh, plus up to ₹1 lakh for mental distress and expenses.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the specific monetary limits for compensation under the scheme.
distractorHooks: Quoting incorrect compensation amounts (e.g., ₹10 lakh, ₹50 lakh).
Stating that the ₹1 lakh for expenses is included within the ₹20 lakh limit.
Claiming there is no upper limit for compensation.
